title: Eva TV Animation Settei Collection 2015
wikitext:
[]: Eva TV Animation Settei Collection 2015             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jun 30, 2015 5:39 pm http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-3860.html http://www.amazon.co.jp/exec/obidos/ASIN/4905033071/apomwvwveg48-22/ref=nosim/ Settei = materials relevant to the production like character, mecha, setting designs, etc. Probably won't have anything we haven't seen before in the dozens of books released in the last 20 years, but hey, now it's all in ONE book! Almost and inch thick at 432 pages and only 3,000 yen. If you've skipping out on all those previous books this might be a good investment. Comes out August 7th. The BD Box coming out later on the 26th can be used as book end if you have too few books to fill up a shelf.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 30, 2015 6:34 pm At 400+ pages, I'm going to hold out some hope that there's something in there we haven't seen before... Certain aspects of NGE settei have always been pretty skimpy, and here's an obvious chance to correct that.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jun 30, 2015 6:50 pm Actually now that I think about it we could get a few more sketches here and there. The 1.0 CRC included a bunch of previously unpublished Sachiel concepts. Maybe the main benefit of this will be BIGGER versions of stuff we've seen before. One example being Kaworu's concept art in 100% Newtype book is tiiiiiiny. New book is A4 sized and needs loads of pages to fill.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 30, 2015 7:09 pm There have actually been two A4-sized collections of NGE settei, which include a good-sized chunk of what was published in Newtype 100%. Put together, they might break 100 pages, and that's with only single-sided printing. Published settei for the films is limited entirely to what's crammed in the back of Groundwork of Movie Vol. 2, and it's hard to believe that's it. Settei for the video versions of 21-24 have never been published anywhere, to my knowledge.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jun 30, 2015 7:46 pm I guess we'll find out around Aug 6th-ish when Japan starts spamming pics of their copies on twitter. But anyways

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Jun 30, 2015 9:03 pm Nnngh, I might have to give in and buy this one. The preview images on Amazon.jp are seriously tempting. I've had serious issues with damaged product buying from Amazon recently, especially where books are concerned. Hopefully I'll have some better luck with the Japanese branch.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 01, 2015 6:39 pm This should be interesting, since it sounds like it'll have new materials based on the page numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by Arturokun951 » Fri Jul 03, 2015 6:53 am Just pre ordered it and its my first time ordering stuff from Japan so i'm not sure how the process will turn out once shipped

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Aug 06, 2015 7:47 pm SHIPPING NOTIFICATION WOOOOOOP WOOOOOOOOOOP

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Aug 06, 2015 8:00 pm Bah! I finally get enough spare cash to order it and now it's unavailable. Edit: Woohoo, back in stock!

[]:             Quote Post by cody727kirby » Fri Aug 07, 2015 1:55 pm This is an awesome deal! How do you guys order from Amazon Japan in US?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Aug 07, 2015 2:18 pm Just go to [url]http://www.amazon.co.jp/[/url]. I'm pretty sure it picks up your existing account info, and if it doesn't always get the language right, use Chrome and tell it to translate. (I use Amazon occasionally in France, Germany, Canada and the US as well, all using my UK login.) But I go to [url]http://www.cdjapan.co.jp/[/url] more often, as it has a properly English version, and usually more of what I want.

[]:             Quote Post by cody727kirby » Fri Aug 07, 2015 2:48 pm Oh, cool. I thought there would be some shipping restrictions or other problems, thank you for the info.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Aug 07, 2015 2:56 pm Some Amazon marketplace sellers won't ship - quite a lot, actually - so there is that. But Amazon themselves do.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Aug 07, 2015 5:18 pm Khara's publishing arm, Groundworks, seems to be very inconsistent at getting things stocked. Maybe they still underestimate demand. The whole in and out of stock thing has already happened with both Q Groundwork books and this seems to follow the pattern. Estimated Delivery: Monday, August 10, 2015 By End of Day

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Aug 07, 2015 8:58 pm pwhodges wrote: Just go to [url]http://www.amazon.co.jp/[/url]. I'm pretty sure it picks up your existing account info, and if it doesn't always get the language right, use Chrome and tell it to translate.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Aug 07, 2015 9:43 pm I'm in Canada. I can't use American amazon.com, it sends me to .ca which usually doesn't have what I want. Canada always gets the shaft like this when it comes to North American retailers. But I can however use amazon.co.jp without any issue and have done so since ugh...2006 I think.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Sat Aug 08, 2015 9:06 am ^Really? That's odd. I'm also in Canada, but I've used the American Amazon.com plenty of times, as well as France's version of Amazon. (Strangely enough, for me it was the Japanese version that didn't let me sign in, until I created a separate account a few years ago with my name written in Japanese. Weird! Maybe they changed their policy later, and that's why everyone else can use it without problems now, heh.) Well, when I go to the American site it asks me if I want to switch to the Canadian one, but I just click on "Stay on Amazon.com" and then it works fine. I wonder why it's forcing you to use the Canadian version? Do you think it varies depending on what province you're in?

[]:             Quote Post by Arturokun951 » Mon Aug 10, 2015 12:28 pm got mine today and holy shit it's amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Aug 10, 2015 12:53 pm ...perhaps I will reconsider buying one. That amazon link is only listing one, though, and it's used...
